rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Right, Title And Interest In Personal Property
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Notice of Hearing has been filed by attorney Boessenecker, who does not have a petition on calendar. 2) Procedural issues have not been addressed by attorney Applegate: a) Declaration of attorney filed on 11/21/19, which identifies the intestate heirs of the estate, has not been verified by Petitioner. It is also unclear who the intestate heirs are, as the declaration does not identify the alleged heirs' relationship to decedent or their lineage. b) As noted previously, the proofs of personal service on annuity companies filed on 10/18/19 do not have attached the Notice of Hearing that was served.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICK OWEN DOHERTY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/25/2020. An amended notice of hearing of the status report including a statement required by Probate Code section 12201(b) regarding the interested parties' right to petition for an account under section 10950 must be served. In addition, a supplemental declaration, signed and verified by the petitioner, addressing the following issues must be filed: (i) All potential successors in interest of Kathryn Joyce Doherty entitled to notice under CRC 7.51(e)(1) must be identified, taking into account the consequences of any no-contest clause in Kathryn Joyce Doherty's trust or will, if applicable; and (ii) An explanation as to why the petitioner needs one year to either close the estate or file an updated status report must be provided.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NESTEL REVOCABLE TRUST, ESTABLISHED FEBRUARY 21, 1997 AND REVOKED ON JULY 1, 2019 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Seeking Court Order Determining The Validity Of Modification/Amendments To Trust Provision
rulings.Rulings: continued to 3/3/2020. the examiner was waiting to hear from all counsel re the continuance request and no courtesy email was sent from 2 attorneys either confirming consent to the continuance or alerting the examiner that consent would not be given. as the examiner was waiting to hear from all counsel, the hearing notes were not submitted in time for the matter to be heard on Wednesday, so it is moved to the next avaiable calendar of 3/3. opposing counsel is requested to email the examiner to either confirm consent to a continuance to 4/22/20 or confirm that no consent will be given so that the matter can be timely set for hearing. deadline for contacting the examiner is noon on thursday, 2/27/20. if no email is received confirming consent will not be given, the matter will be continued to 4/22/20.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARITSA CALDERON SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Bring Trust Under Probate Department Supervisor; For Authorization To Purchase Accessible Vehicle; To Pay Care Giving Fees; And To Approve
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Bond was required to be filed in the amount of $128,000 within 60 days of the order approving compromise of claim. The order was filed on 2/14/19 but the bond was only recently filed on 2/3/20. Per 12/23/19 declaration, attorney indicates that she previously attempted to file the bond but was rejected, as there was no order to file bond. The rejection is dated 9/3/19, which was more than 7 months after order approving claim was filed. 2) Trustee requests that this special needs trust be brought under the supervision of the probate court; 3) Court to determine whether request to purchase van should be granted. 4) Court to determine whether request to provide caregiving services should be granted. Trustee has not addressed duty of parents to care for their child. 5) Attorney requests $1,969 to prepare this petition.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOHN ARCHIE MERRILL & ANNE M. MERRILL JOINT LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/08/2020. No notes will be sent. The following issues must be resolved: (1) According to the proof of service (filed 01/29/2020), the second amended notice of hearing was served on 01/27/2020, but the amended petition was not filed until 01/29/2020. Notice may not be mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. LRSF 14.21A. (2) A supplemental declaration, signed and verified by at least one of the petitioners, addressing the following issues, must be filed and served (CRC 7.53(b)): (i) Amended Schedule A (Exhibit C attached to the amended petition) refers to 04/19/1995 as the date of the joint trust, but according to paragraph 1 of the amended petition and first trust amendment (Exhibits B), the trust date is 04/21/1995. (ii) Amended Schedule A refers to the "Survivor's" Trust and "Bypass" Trust as subtrusts created under the joint trust, but the trust agreement does not refer to any subtrusts by those names. (iii) Amended Schedule A was created after Ann Merrill died and was only signed by John Merrill. Prior to her death, did Ann Merrill show her intent to transfer the assets in question to the joint trust?
